title: Cotyledon gaditana
intro: Cotyledon gaditana, also known as the Gaditanum or Rockrose, is a succulent plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its thick, waxy leaves and vibrant red, yellow, or orange flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cotyledon gaditana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cotyledon gaditana is often used in preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Cotyledon gaditana is classified under the family Crassulaceae, commonly known as the stonecrop or orpine family. This family comprises a diverse group of succulent plants, many of which are popular for their ornamental value. The genus Cotyledon, within this family, includes species native to various parts of the world, including the Iberian Peninsula, where Cotyledon gaditana is found. Cotyledon gaditana is often characterized by its fleshy leaves and distinctive growth habit. The classification of Cotyledon gaditana within the Crassulaceae family reflects its shared characteristics and evolutionary history with other succulent species.
